messages.content:
**Academic Writing Exercises: Paraphrasing and Avoiding Plagiarism**
**1.1 quoting statistics**
Which of the statistics and findings in bold would you be able to use in your paper without making a reference to the literature or attributing a source?
The growing interest in bilingual or multilingual speakers is not surprising if we think that more than half of the world’s population – (1) **about 3.5 billion people** – regularly speak (2) **more than one language** (Grosjean, 1982, 2010). As far as Europe is concerned, the European Commission recently published a report (2006) in which a large sample of European citizens were asked how many languages they spoke other than their mother tongue. (3) **Fifty-six percent of the people in 25 countries** replied that they could have a conversation in a second language, and 28% replied they spoke a third. (4) **Great Britain is one of the most “monolingual” countries in Europe**; nonetheless, (5) **38% of those polled** replied they could speak a second language.
These figures, though impressive, do not tell us much about a potentially bilingual or multilingual population that appears to be a tremendously heterogeneous group. Were the languages learnt early in childhood or later? Are the additional languages used in everyday life? How competent are these people in their second language? These (6) **three basic questions** are themselves enough to transmit even to the naïve eye how difficult studying bilinguals could be. As Grosjean (1998) pointed out “…working with bilinguals is a more challenging enterprise [than studying monolinguals].
**Answer:**
**1.2 paraphrasing by changing nouns into verbs**
Replace the phrases in italics with a verb and make any other necessary changes.
The presence of mixtures of saccharide materials _make the identification of a plant gum in a paint sample a difficult task._
= The presence of mixtures of saccharide materials make it difficult to identify a plant gum in a paint sample.
1.     _The assessment of this index was carried out_ by means of the correlation function.
**Answer:**
2.     _The heating of the probe can be carried out_ in two different ways:
**Answer:**
3.     The main drawbacks are the increase in volume and weight of the residue which _causes the loss of the advantage of_ the incineration process, and _the production of a material_ that might still be very hazardous for the environment.
**Answer:**
4.     This solution _implies the reaching of a consensus among_ these processes.
**Answer:**
5.     The authors wish to thank the Department of Political Sciences _for the setting up and coordination of the project_.
**Answer:**
**1.3 finding synonyms**
Write at least one synonym for each of the words/phrases in bold.
1.     Vitous [2015] has **provided** a new definition, in which...
**Answer:**
2.     He **claims** that...
**Answer:**
3.     No significant difference was **revealed** between the two methods.
**Answer:**
4.     Having this system **enabled us to** incorporate several new parameters.
**Answer:**
5.     Figure 1 **presents** the data on the new system.
**Answer:**
6.     The main **criteria** for selecting the samples was not mentioned at all.
**Answer:**
7.     In the first **step** of the process...
**Answer:**
8.     Malaria is the **main** cause of...
**Answer:**
9.     The samples were prepared **as described by** Jude [2010].
**Answer:**
10.  The **traditional** approach to sample collection is to...
**Answer:**
**1.4 paraphrasing by changing word order**
Rewrite these sentences by putting the part in bold at the beginning of the sentence. Make changes if necessary.
1.     There are three categories of **rendering techniques**. These are A, B, and C.
**Answer:**
2.     It is still not fully understood **how the brain works**.
**Answer:**
3.     The usual length of **the rod** is two meters.
**Answer:**
**1.5 making a summary**
Write a 10–20 word summary of the following text, which comes from a freely available NASA document on writing technical reports.
You will be given, say, 20 minutes in which to present to visiting scientists a review of your research. Your first reaction is to insist that you need more than 20 minutes. Your arguments will be rejected, however, so you eventually proceed complainingly to your task. With great ingenuity you apply yourself to outwit your stubborn and arbitrary boss by getting an hour’s material into a 20-minute talk. You concoct long and brilliantly comprehensive sentences for your discussion, you organize all of your numerical results so that you can present them in rapid-fire order, and you lay out 15 slides, each crammed with detailed information. Unfortunately, when you first read through your prepared speech, it takes nearly 30 minutes instead of 20. You are not very disturbed, however, because you are confident that, with a little practice, you will be able to increase your speaking rate until the delivery time is down to the required 20 minutes.
All that is missing from your approach is consideration for the central figure – the man in the audience. After 2 minutes of your talk he will be rapidly developing mental indigestion; after five minutes he will have lost the thread of your discussion; and during the remainder of your talk he will simply concentrate on hating you or on trying to sleep.
**Answer:**
  
just give me the final answer

## 1.1 Quoting statistics — which can be used with no reference?
**Only (6) “three basic questions.”**  
All the other bold items **(1)–(5)** are specific statistics/findings and **must be referenced**.

---

## 1.2 Paraphrasing by changing nouns into verbs
1. **This index was assessed** by means of the correlation function.  
2. **The probe can be heated** in two different ways:  
3. The main drawbacks are the increase in volume and weight of the residue, which **causes us to lose the advantage** of the incineration process, and **produces a material** that might still be very hazardous for the environment.  
4. This solution **implies reaching a consensus among** these processes.  
5. The authors wish to thank the Department of Political Sciences **for setting up and coordinating the project**.

---

## 1.3 Finding synonyms
1. **provided** → *offered / proposed*  
2. **claims** → *argues / asserts*  
3. **revealed** → *showed / demonstrated*  
4. **enabled us to** → *allowed us to / made it possible for us to*  
5. **presents** → *shows / illustrates*  
6. **criteria** → *standards / requirements*  
7. **step** → *stage / phase*  
8. **main** → *primary / principal*  
9. **as described by** → *as outlined by / according to*  
10. **traditional** → *conventional / established*

---

## 1.4 Paraphrasing by changing word order
1. **Rendering techniques** fall into three categories: A, B, and C.  
2. **How the brain works** is still not fully understood.  
3. **The rod** is usually two meters long.

---

## 1.5 10–20 word summary
Overloading a short talk with detail ignores audience limits and quickly loses listeners.

**Translation**
_Complete the sentences by translating into English the Chinese given in brackets with the structure or phrases in the table below._
| account for | on the basis of | a risk to |
| --- | --- | --- |
| a sharp reduction in | vary from … to … | suitable for |
1) The special chemical property of nitrogen ___________________ (使之适用于食品包装) and producing chemicals safely.
2) Extremely dry and hot weather has caused ________________ (作物产量的急剧下降) in that country.
3) It is reported that suicide deaths ___________________ (占到全部非自然死亡人数的一半以上) in the world.
4) The project design will be evaluated ______________________ (基于可行性和实用价值).
5) Heavy metal pollution in soil not only affects growth and yield of crop plants, but also  ____________________ (对公众健康产生潜在的风险).
6) The current Ebola outbreak has seen that ______________________ (生存率在一个地方和另一个地方之间有很大的差别) and depends greatly on the type of care patients receive.
  
just give me the final answer

1) The special chemical property of nitrogen **makes it suitable for food packaging** and producing chemicals safely.  

2) Extremely dry and hot weather has caused **a sharp reduction in crop yields** in that country.  

3) It is reported that suicide deaths **account for more than half of all unnatural deaths** in the world.  

4) The project design will be evaluated **on the basis of feasibility and practical value**.  

5) Heavy metal pollution in soil not only affects growth and yield of crop plants, but also **poses a potential risk to public health**.  

6) The current Ebola outbreak has seen that **survival rates vary greatly from one place to another** and depend greatly on the type of care patients receive.

**Pre-watching task**
_Work together with your partner and fill in the following table with the suggestions on sustainable lifestyle. Your suggestions should be as specific as possible. Try to come up with a creative list with your partner._
| **What can we do** | **Purpose** | **Why is it sustainable** |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Eg. Using LED light bulb | Saving electricity | LED light bulb can emit more light but with less electricity consumption |
| 1) |  |  |
| 2) |  |  |
| 3) |  |  |

| **What can we do** | **Purpose** | **Why is it sustainable** |
| --- | --- | --- |
| 1) Carry a reusable water bottle and coffee cup | Reduce single-use plastic/paper waste | Cuts demand for disposable items and lowers landfill waste and production emissions |
| 2) Plan meals and compost food scraps | Reduce food waste | Prevents methane from landfills and returns nutrients to soil, reducing need for chemical fertilizers |
| 3) Use public transport/bike/walk for short trips (or carpool) | Reduce fuel use and air pollution | Lowers CO₂ emissions per person and reduces traffic congestion and resource consumption |

**Build a green house**

**Spot dictation**
_In this task, you’ll listen to part of the video clip again. While listening, fill in the  blanks with the exact words you hear from the clip._ 
And so, like many people, we start with a house on a lot, and I'm going to show you a typical 1) ___________ on the top and what we're doing on the bottom. So first, we 2) ___________ it. It takes some energy, but if you deconstruct it -- you take it all apart, you use the bits -- you can get some of that energy back. We then dug a big hole to put in a rainwater catchment 3) ___________ to take our yard water independent. And then we poured a big 4) __________ for passive solar. Now, you can 5) _________ the embodied energy by about 25 percent by using high fly ash concrete. We then put in framing. And so this is framing -- lumber, 6) _________ materials -- and it's kind of hard to get the embodied energy out of that, but it can be a 7) _________ resource if you use FSC-certified lumber.
We then go on to the first thing that was very surprising. If we put aluminum windows in this house, we would 8) _________ the energy use right there. Now, PVC is a little bit better, but still not as good as the wood that we chose. We then put in plumbing, electrical and HVAC, and 9) __________. Now, spray foam is an excellent insulator -- it fills in all the cracks -- but it is pretty high embodied energy, and, sprayed-in cellulose or blue jeans is a much lower energy 10) _________ to that. We also used straw bale infill for our library, which has zero embodied energy.
**Post-watching task**
_Suppose you are given a chance to design and build your dream house of a green building. Work in groups of four and list the projects you would like to do. Then explain why these projects will help to make your house a green one._   
| **Projects** | **Justifications** |
| --- | --- |
| Eg. Using high efficient heat insulating material in walls and roofs | Heat insulating walls and roofs can keep the house much cooler in summer and warmer in winter, which will save much energy in daily life. |
|  |  |
| 完成**Post-watching task 这一 part** |  |
|  |  |

| **Projects** | **Justifications** |
| --- | --- |
| Install rooftop solar panels + home battery | Generates clean electricity on-site, reduces reliance on fossil-fuel power, lowers long-term energy use and bills. |
| Build a rainwater harvesting system (tank + filtration) for toilets/garden | Cuts municipal water demand and uses free rainfall, improving water security and reducing stormwater runoff. |
| Use passive design: south-facing windows, shading, thermal mass, cross-ventilation | Reduces heating/cooling needs by using sunlight and natural airflow, lowering energy consumption year-round. |
| Use high-performance insulation and airtight windows/doors (double/triple glazing) | Minimizes heat loss in winter and heat gain in summer, so HVAC runs less and saves energy. |
| Choose low-carbon/recycled materials (FSC wood, recycled steel, fly-ash concrete) | Lowers embodied energy and carbon footprint of construction; supports responsible sourcing. |
| Install efficient appliances and smart controls (LEDs, smart thermostat, motion sensors) | Reduces unnecessary electricity use through automation and high-efficiency equipment. |
| Create a green roof/vertical garden and plant native trees | Improves insulation, reduces urban heat, increases biodiversity, and helps manage rainwater. |
| Add a composting + recycling station and a small home garden | Reduces household waste, cuts methane from landfills, and provides local food with lower transport emissions. |

**Pre-reading task**
_Wind has been used to provide energy since ancient times. Nowadays, wind energy is believed to be one important form of green energy. Work with your partner and discuss the following questions._
1) As far as you know, how has wind been used to provide energy for people?
2) Wind energy is regarded as green energy. What are the advantages and disadvantages of wind energy in terms of the impacts on environment? 
**Wind Energy**         
Wind energy is believed to be the most advanced of the “new” renewable energy technologies. Since 2001, wind power has been growing at a phenomenal rate of 20% to 30% per annum. Wind power (2016 GW) is expected to provide 12% of the global electricity by 2050, thereby avoiding annually 2.8 gigatonnes of emissions of CO2 equivalent. This would need an investment of USD 3.2 trillion during 2010–2050. Atmospheric scientists are developing highly localized weather forecasts to enable the utility companies to know when to power up the wind turbines.
Wind turbines do not need gusty winds; they need only moderate but steady winds. Wind turbines start producing electricity when the wind speed reaches 18–25 km/hr (5 to 7 m/s), reaching their rated output when the wind speed reaches about 47 km/hr (13 m/s). So any area where the wind speeds are greater than about 18 km/hr (5 m/s) is suitable for generating wind electricity, and such areas are plentiful. When the wind speeds exceed 22 to 26 m/s, the turbine is shut off to avoid damage to the structure.
Availability of wind turbine is defined as the proportion of the time that it is ready for use. Operation and maintenance costs are determined by this factor. Availability varies from 97% onshore to 80–95% offshore.
Improved turbine design is aimed at extracting more energy from the wind, more of the time, and over longer period of time. Affordable materials with higher strength-to-mass ratio are needed for the purpose. More power is captured by having a larger area through which the turbine can extract energy (the swept area of the rotor), and installing the rotor at a greater height (to take advantage of the rapidly moving air).
A typical 2MW wind turbine has two or three blades, each about 40m long, and made of fiberglass or composite material. The nacelle, which is the housing on the top of the tower, contains the generator and gearbox to convert the rotational energy into electricity. The tower height is about 80 m. The largest wind turbines presently in operation in the world, has a capacity of 5–6MW each, with rotor diameter up to 126 m.
ENVIRONMENTAL FACTORS
The plus point of the wind power is that it has no carbon dioxide emissions. However, wind power has three environmental impacts: visual impact, noise, risk of bird collisions and disruption of wild life. The best places for siting wind farms are tops of hills, bluffs along the open ocean and areas, which are not obstructed by topography. But these happen to be the very places, which people cherish for their scenic beauty. Two kinds of noises are associated with wind turbines: **aerodynamic noise1** from the blades, and mechanical noise from the rotating machinery. Design improvements are bringing about a sharp reduction in the noise. The risk to migratory birds could be avoided by siting the wind farms where the routes of the migratory birds do not cross.
COSTS AND MARKETS
Wind power is capital intensive with capital costs accounting for 75–80% of the production costs – the corresponding figure for fossil fuel power stations is 40–60%. The onshore wind power production costs depend upon the wind conditions – they are low in areas of high wind speeds (such as, coastal areas) and high in areas of low wind speeds (such as, inland areas).
There are wind farms in about 40 countries in the world, with thirteen of them having a capacity of 1 000MW of installed capacity.
In 1980, Denmark and California were virtually the only markets in the world for wind turbines. The market collapsed in California when the financial incentives were withdrawn. Denmark survived by falling on the stable domestic market. In mid-1990’s, Germany entered the market, followed by Spain. There has been a great boom in the wind power industry. Six leading turbine manufacturers account for 90% of the global market.
Three parameters determine the amount of power from wind turbine: wind conditions, turbine height, and efficiency of the turbine. While the wind regime2 of a site is a given, higher output of power can be realized by making the turbines larger and taller. Germany and Denmark increased the productivity of their prime sites by replacing the earlier-installed smaller and shorter turbines by larger and taller turbines. The efficiency of energy production is measured on the basis of annual energy production per unit of swept rotor area (kWh/m2). The same parameter determines the manufacturing costs. The trend is therefore towards larger and taller and more efficient wind turbines. The efficiency of the wind power sector has increased by 2–3% annually during the last 15 years through better turbine siting, more efficient equipment and higher hub heights.
_Notes:_
1. aerodynamic noise: Noise resulting from the flow of air空气动力噪音
2. Wind regime: 风况
**Reading Comprehension**
_1. Find out how the following factors influence the power production efficiency and power production costs, and decide whether these factors have any environmental impacts._
| **Factors** | **Efficiency** | **Costs** | **Environment impacts** |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Building larger and taller turbine |  |  |  |
| Locating the wind farm along seashore |  |  |  |
| Designing low-noise and high-efficiency turbine |  |  |  |
**完成Pre-reading task和Reading Comprehension**

## Pre-reading task

### 1) How has wind been used to provide energy for people?
- **Sailing ships/boats:** wind in sails provides propulsion for transport and trade.  
- **Windmills for milling grain:** wind turns blades to grind wheat/corn into flour.  
- **Pumping water:** wind-powered pumps lift water for irrigation and livestock.  
- **Modern wind turbines:** convert wind’s kinetic energy into **electricity** for homes and industry.

### 2) Advantages and disadvantages of wind energy (environmental impacts)
**Advantages**
- **No CO₂ emissions during operation** (helps reduce greenhouse gases and air pollution).
- **Renewable** and reduces dependence on fossil fuels.
- Uses little water compared with thermal power plants.

**Disadvantages**
- **Visual impact** on scenic landscapes.
- **Noise** (aerodynamic noise from blades + mechanical noise).
- **Bird/bat collisions** and possible wildlife disruption (can be reduced by better siting/design).

---

## Reading Comprehension (table)

| **Factors** | **Efficiency** | **Costs** | **Environment impacts** |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| **Building larger and taller turbine** | **Increases efficiency/output** (bigger swept area; higher hub reaches faster winds; more energy captured more of the time) | **Often increases upfront capital/manufacturing costs**, but can **lower cost per kWh** because of higher annual energy production | **Possible higher visual impact**; may also affect wildlife/landscape depending on siting |
| **Locating the wind farm along seashore** | **Higher efficiency** because coastal areas usually have **higher/steadier winds** | **Lower production costs** in high-wind areas (text: costs low in high wind speeds) | Potential **visual impact** on coast scenery; possible **wildlife (birds) impacts**; noise concerns for nearby communities |
| **Designing low-noise and high-efficiency turbine** | **Increases efficiency** (more efficient equipment extracts more energy) | May **increase design/manufacturing cost**, but can **reduce operating issues** and improve overall economics | **Reduces noise pollution** (sharp reduction in noise); may indirectly reduce conflict with people and improve siting options |
